questions.question: Describe the latitudinal and longitudinal extent of India.
questions.answer: India extends between 8°4'N and 37°6'N latitudes and between 68°7'E and 97°25'E longitudes.

questions.question: Explain the significance of the Tropic of Cancer for India.
questions.answer: The Tropic of Cancer divides India into tropical and subtropical climatic regions and influences climate and vegetation.

questions.question: Explain the concept of Indian Standard Time.
questions.answer: Indian Standard Time is based on 82°30'E longitude passing near Mirzapur and ensures uniform time across India.

questions.question: Why does the sun rise earlier in Arunachal Pradesh than in Gujarat?
questions.answer: Arunachal Pradesh lies in the easternmost part of India, so sunrise occurs earlier than in Gujarat which lies in the west.

questions.question: Explain the physical boundaries of India.
questions.answer: India is bounded by the Himalayas in the north and surrounded by the Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal and Indian Ocean.

questions.question: Explain the importance of India’s central location in Asia.
questions.answer: India's central location helped establish trade and cultural contacts with many regions.

questions.question: Describe the role of land routes in India's contacts with the world.
questions.answer: Land routes through mountain passes helped travellers and traders reach India.

questions.question: Explain the importance of oceans for India's trade.
questions.answer: Oceans enabled maritime trade connecting India with many regions of the world.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Indian Ocean for India.
questions.answer: The Indian Ocean connects India with international sea routes and enhances trade and cultural contacts.

questions.question: Explain why India is called a subcontinent.
questions.answer: India is called a subcontinent because of its large size and distinct geographical identity.

questions.question: Explain how the opening of the Suez Canal helped India.
questions.answer: The Suez Canal reduced the distance between India and Europe by about 7,000 km.

questions.question: Describe the goods traded from India in ancient times.
questions.answer: India exported spices, muslin and other goods to many parts of the world.

questions.question: Explain how ideas spread from India to other countries.
questions.answer: Ideas such as the decimal system and Indian numerals spread through trade and cultural contacts.

questions.question: Describe India's neighbouring countries.
questions.answer: India shares borders with Pakistan, Afghanistan, China, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Myanmar.

questions.question: Explain the island neighbours of India.
questions.answer: Sri Lanka and Maldives are island neighbours of India.

questions.question: Explain how Sri Lanka is separated from India.
questions.answer: Sri Lanka is separated from India by the Palk Strait and Gulf of Mannar.

questions.question: Describe the location of the Maldives Islands.
questions.answer: Maldives lie south of Lakshadweep Islands in the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Describe the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
questions.answer: They lie in the Bay of Bengal and form a major island group of India.

questions.question: Describe the Lakshadweep Islands.
questions.answer: Lakshadweep Islands lie in the Arabian Sea and are coral islands.

questions.question: Explain the importance of India's coastline.
questions.answer: India's coastline supports maritime trade, fishing and port development.

questions.question: Explain the importance of trade routes for India.
questions.answer: Trade routes helped India exchange goods, culture and ideas with other civilizations.

questions.question: Explain why India has diverse cultural influences.
questions.answer: Because India had contacts with many civilizations through trade and migration.

questions.question: Explain the geographical diversity of India.
questions.answer: India's large area results in diverse landforms, climates and cultures.

questions.question: Explain why India has strategic importance in South Asia.
questions.answer: India's central location and connections with neighbouring countries give it regional importance.

questions.question: Explain the overall importance of India's geographical location.
questions.answer: India's location helps in trade, cultural exchange and strategic relations with many countries.
